familyOpenStatement: FAMILY-OPEN: The draft (Section 8) leaves Shiab as an underdetermined family — the author states the family has 'a byzantine taxonomy' and the operator choice is intentionally underdetermined. Three-operator comparison: standard(identity-shiab) vs paired(first-order-curvature): identity-equivalent (max|diff|=0); standard vs metric-scaled-shiab: distinct (max|diff|=0.3); paired vs metric-scaled-shiab: max|diff|=0.3. The third operator (metric-scaled-shiab) is numerically distinct from the standard (max|diff|=0.3, max|output|=0.3). This constitutes a partial Shiab family expansion on the current toy geometry (dimX=2). On dimX=2, Lambda^2(T*X) is 1-dimensional; the only distinguishable Shiab variant is scalar scaling. This does NOT change the branch robustness conclusion — scalar rescaling of F shifts the solution uniformly and preserves gauge-violation and solver-iteration fragility behavior. Full Shiab family exploration (Ricci/Weyl decompositions) requires dimX >= 4 per physicist guidance. None of the exercised operators is the canonically selected draft operator. All current branches carry draftAlignmentStatus=surrogate. The Shiab family remains open pending draft-aligned 4D geometry and physics guidance.
